headline: James is quieter in team rituals because he lacks clear, recognized criteria after the promotion decision—so he’s redirecting into mentoring without confidence in the payoff.
summary_bullets: James links his post-decision shift to uncertainty: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.”
He internalized feedback as controllable delivery, not recognition: “I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.”
Mentoring feels useful but mismatched to recognition: “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
understanding_paragraph: James is less present in team rituals since the promotion decision, and he attributes it to not knowing what needed to change. He’s trying to stay effective by focusing on delivery, but he doubts whether his current work (including mentoring) maps to what gets recognized. He wants a concrete, criteria-driven path with real authority to commit to bigger direction.

axes.meaning: Mild strain shows up as reduced ritual presence; worth watching over the next few weeks.
Quiet disengagement from rituals, with motivation expressed through usefulness rather than ownership.
He has a workable direction request: define the path precisely and name the forum for criteria.
A real growth drag: he doesn’t trust that mentoring work connects to recognition, so stretch may not be landing.

brutal_truth_employee: James ties his dip directly to uncertainty: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.” He’s also explicit that his current effort may not be rewarded: “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
brutal_truth_manager: When James said, “I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable,” you got the thread into mentoring, but you didn’t press on what “criteria” he believes was missing from the promotion feedback. The moment to deepen was his request for “clearer criteria, not reassurance,” because that’s the concrete gap to translate into observable expectations.

next_actions.action: Confirm with James which specific forum he named for clearer criteria, and schedule a 30-minute working session to define the criteria together.
Set up a short check-in with James to map mentoring activities to recognition criteria (what gets credited, by whom, and how success is evidenced).
Re-ask the “define the path precisely” question with a concrete output: ask James to bring one draft criteria list and the authority boundaries he needs.

watch_for: Within two weeks, if James’s ritual presence stays low while he repeats “not sure the work…gets recognized,” treat the criteria/recognition mismatch as unresolved.
Before next 1:1, if James can name a specific forum and concrete criteria without prompting, that confirms clarity is improving and engagement should follow.
